title: 10 Herbal Teas to Ease Jaundice Symptoms
intro_desc: Herbal teas for jaundice are natural remedies derived from plants that help alleviate the symptoms of this liver disorder by promoting liver detoxification and improving bile flow. The best herbal teas for jaundice include Silybum marianum, Artemisia absinthium, Cynara scolymus, Taraxacum officinale, Glycyrrhiza glabra, Curcuma longa, Zingiber officinale, Cassia auriculata, Cichorium intybus, and Cassia angustifolia. If left untreated, jaundice can lead to fatigue, nausea, dark urine, and yellowing skin, impacting one's quality of life, daily activities, and overall well-being, ultimately affecting relationships and work productivity.

remedies.remedy_desc: Silybum marianum tea, also known as milk thistle tea, is used to treat jaundice. Jaundice is a condition where the liver doesn't work properly, causing a buildup of a yellow pigment called bilirubin in the blood. This can be very serious, especially in newborns. Silybum marianum tea contains a compound called silymarin, which helps protect and repair liver cells. When liver cells are damaged, silymarin can help them recover and function properly again. This can reduce the amount of bilirubin in the blood, making jaundice symptoms less severe. Silybum marianum tea also has anti-inflammatory properties, which can help reduce swelling and pain in the liver. As a result, it can help alleviate symptoms of jaundice, such as yellowing of the skin and eyes, and improve overall liver function. Silybum marianum tea has been used for centuries to treat liver problems, and its effectiveness has been studied in several scientific trials.

remedy_constituents.constituent_desc: Silymarin is good for jaundice because it helps remove bilirubin from the blood, a yellow pigment that builds up and causes jaundice, by increasing liver function and reducing inflammation.

Flavonoids are good for jaundice because they have anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce liver inflammation and improve liver function, which in turn helps to remove bilirubin and other waste products from the blood, reducing the symptoms of jaundice.

Lignans are good for jaundice because they have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that help to reduce liver inflammation and improve liver function, which in turn helps to break down bilirubin and reduce jaundice symptoms.

Phenolic acids are good for jaundice because they have antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce liver inflammation and improve bile flow, thereby alleviating the symptoms of jaundice.

Polyphenols are good for jaundice because they have anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce liver inflammation, thereby improving liver function and facilitating the removal of bilirubin, which is the main cause of jaundice.

remedies.image_alt: Silybum marianum herbal teas for jaundice
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make Bupleurum chinense herbal tea to help with jaundice, start by gathering dried Bupleurum chinense roots. You can buy these at a local herbal store or online. Rinse the dried roots under cold water to remove any dust or debris. Then, place one teaspoon of the roots in a cup and add boiling water to fill the cup. Let it steep for 5-7 minutes, allowing the roots to release their medicinal properties into the water. After the steeping time has passed, strain the liquid into another cup to remove the roots. Discard the roots and let the tea cool down to a drinkable temperature. Once cooled, you can drink the tea as needed to help alleviate jaundice symptoms. It's recommended to consult with a healthcare professional before using this tea, especially if you're pregnant or breastfeeding, as Bupleurum chinense may interact with certain medications or worsen certain health conditions.

remedies.remedy_desc: Glycyrrhiza glabra tea, also known as licorice root tea, is a traditional remedy for jaundice. Jaundice is a condition where the skin and eyes turn yellow due to a buildup of a yellow pigment called bilirubin in the blood. This buildup can be caused by various factors, including liver disease or bile duct blockages. Glycyrrhiza glabra tea has been used to help reduce inflammation and improve liver function. The active compounds in the tea, such as glycyrrhizin, have been shown to have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which can help soothe the liver and reduce the levels of bilirubin in the blood. By reducing inflammation and improving liver function, Glycyrrhiza glabra tea may help alleviate the symptoms of jaundice and promote the body's natural healing processes. Additionally, the tea has been used to support the body's natural detoxification processes, which can help remove toxins and excess bilirubin from the body. Regular consumption of Glycyrrhiza glabra tea may also help to prevent jaundice from occurring in the first place.

remedy_constituents.constituent_desc: Glycyrrhizin is good for jaundice because it helps to reduce liver inflammation and improve bile flow, thereby allowing the liver to function more efficiently and remove bilirubin, the main cause of jaundice.

Triterpenoids are good for jaundice because they have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that help to reduce liver damage and promote the production of bile, which can help to clear the yellow pigment responsible for jaundice.

remedies.remedy_preparation: To make Bupleurum chinense herbal tea that helps with jaundice, start by gathering high-quality dried Bupleurum root. This herb is known for its liver-protecting properties and is often used in traditional Chinese medicine. Begin by boiling 1 teaspoon of the dried root in 1 cup of water for 5-7 minutes to release its medicinal properties. You can also add other herbs like Scuteellaria baicalensis and Glycyrrhiza uralensis to enhance its effectiveness. After boiling, let the tea steep for 5 minutes before straining it into a cup. You can add honey to taste if desired. It's recommended to drink 2-3 cups of this tea per day to help alleviate jaundice symptoms. However, please consult a healthcare professional before using Bupleurum chinense herbal tea, especially if you have any underlying medical conditions or are pregnant/breastfeeding.

remedies.remedy_desc: Taraxacum officinale, also known as dandelion, is a plant that has been used for centuries to treat various health issues, including jaundice. Jaundice is a condition where the liver is not working properly, causing a buildup of bilirubin in the blood, which can turn the skin and eyes yellow. Dandelion tea can help to treat jaundice by supporting the liver's function. The tea is rich in antioxidants and flavonoids, which have anti-inflammatory properties that can help to reduce liver inflammation and promote liver cell regeneration. Additionally, dandelion tea contains inulin, a type of fiber that can help to stimulate the liver and gallbladder to produce bile, which can help to break down bilirubin and remove it from the body. Drinking dandelion tea may also help to improve digestion, reduce nausea and vomiting, and alleviate fatigue, which are common symptoms of jaundice.

Tannins are good for jaundice because they help reduce liver inflammation and improve liver function by inhibiting the production of bile and increasing the flow of bile, which helps to clear the body of toxins and reduce yellowing of the skin and eyes.
Polyphenols are good for jaundice because they have anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce liver inflammation and improve liver function, which can help to alleviate jaundice symptoms.

remedies.remedy_preparation: To make Bupleurum chinense herbal tea for jaundice relief, start by gathering the dried roots of the Bupleurum chinense plant. Rinse the roots gently with cold water, then chop them into small pieces. In a large pot, combine 1 tablespoon of the chopped roots with 2 cups of boiling water. Reduce the heat to a simmer and let the mixture steep for 10-15 minutes. Strain the liquid through a cheesecloth or a fine-mesh sieve into a separate container, discarding the solids. Let the tea cool before serving. The recommended dosage is 1 cup of tea, 2-3 times a day. It's essential to note that Bupleurum chinense tea should not be consumed for extended periods without consulting a healthcare professional, and individuals with liver or kidney problems should avoid it altogether.

Bupleurum chinense herbal tea is traditionally used to help alleviate symptoms of jaundice, a condition characterized by yellowing of the skin and eyes due to liver dysfunction. When consumed regularly, the tea may help to stimulate liver function and promote the removal of bile, potentially reducing the severity of jaundice symptoms. It's essential to consult with a healthcare professional before using Bupleurum chinense herbal tea, especially if you have a pre-existing medical condition or are taking medications.

supplementary_best: To remedy jaundice, a combination of herbal teas made from 'Silybum marianum', 'Bupleurum chinense', and 'Curcuma longa' is effective. 'Silybum marianum', also known as milk thistle, helps to repair liver cells damaged by toxins, thereby reducing jaundice symptoms. 'Bupleurum chinense' supports liver function and boosts the body's natural detoxification processes. 'Curcuma longa', or turmeric, has potent anti-inflammatory properties that help to reduce liver inflammation and promote healing. This combination is the best because it addresses the root cause of jaundice, which is liver damage, and provides multiple benefits to the liver and body. The teas should be consumed in moderation and in combination with other treatments, as advised by a healthcare professional.
supplementary_avoid: If you have jaundice, you should avoid herbal teas that can worsen liver problems or interact with medications. One such herb is 'Dandelion root' (Taraxacum officinale, is excluded, but, for example) 'Gentiana lutea' and 'Ginkgo biloba' teas. These herbs can stimulate the liver and kidneys to work harder, but they can also cause problems if the liver is already damaged. 'Ginkgo biloba' can also thin the blood and increase the risk of bleeding, which can be a concern if you're taking medications for jaundice. Another herb to avoid is 'Cleavers' (Galium aparine), which can cause the liver to work harder and may interact with certain medications. 'Echinacea' teas should also be avoided, as they can stimulate the immune system and worsen liver inflammation. 'Dandelion leaf' (Taraxacum officinale, is excluded, but, for example) 'Goldenseal' (Hydrastis canadensis) teas should be avoided as they can cause the liver to work harder. 'Yarrow' (Achillea millefolium) and 'Valerian root' (Valeriana officinalis) teas should also be avoided as they can interact with certain medications and worsen liver problems.
supplementary_other_remedies: For jaundice, some people use natural remedies like activated charcoal. It helps get rid of the yellow pigment bilirubin from the body, which is what causes jaundice. When taken orally, activated charcoal can bind to bilirubin in the digestive system and help remove it from the body. This can help reduce the symptoms of jaundice, like yellow skin and eyes.  Another remedy is turmeric. Turmeric contains a compound called curcumin, which has anti-inflammatory properties. Curcumin can help reduce swelling and inflammation in the liver, which can be helpful when treating jaundice caused by liver damage. It can also help improve liver function and reduce the levels of bilirubin in the blood.  Some people also use milk thistle oil. Milk thistle is a plant that contains a compound called silymarin, which helps protect the liver from damage. Silymarin can help repair liver cells and improve liver function, which can be helpful when treating jaundice caused by liver damage. It can also help reduce the levels of bilirubin in the blood.  Ginger is another natural remedy that can help with jaundice. Ginger has anti-inflammatory properties, which can help reduce swelling and inflammation in the liver. It can also help improve digestion and reduce nausea, which are common symptoms of jaundice. Ginger can be taken as a tea or in its raw form.  Other natural remedies, like clay and bentonite, can also help remove toxins from the body. These remedies can help bind to bilirubin and other toxins in the digestive system, making it easier for the body to remove them.
supplementary_related_ailments: People who have jaundice often experience other health issues. One common problem is liver damage. This is because jaundice is usually caused by liver problems, such as hepatitis or cirrhosis. Medicinal herbs like milk thistle and dandelion root can help heal liver damage. Milk thistle contains a compound called silymarin, which protects liver cells and promotes healing. Dandelion root has antioxidants that reduce inflammation and promote liver function.  Another issue people with jaundice may face is gallstones. These are hard deposits that form in the gallbladder and can block bile flow, leading to jaundice. Turmeric and ginger can help dissolve gallstones and improve bile flow. Turmeric contains a compound called curcumin, which has anti-inflammatory properties and helps break down gallstones. Ginger has natural digestive enzymes that aid in bile digestion and flow.  People with jaundice may also experience digestive issues, such as diarrhea or constipation. This is because the liver's ability to digest and process food is impaired. Licorice root and slippery elm can help soothe and protect the digestive tract. Licorice root has anti-inflammatory properties that reduce inflammation in the digestive tract, while slippery elm forms a protective barrier that prevents irritation.  Some people with jaundice may experience fatigue and weakness. This is because the liver is not producing enough red blood cells. Ashwagandha and ginseng can help improve energy levels and reduce fatigue. Ashwagandha is an adaptogen that helps the body cope with stress and promotes energy production, while ginseng has natural stimulants that improve energy and vitality.
